title: Long Bennington III Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Plt Off. Cedric Arthur Chapman

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of the loss of Lancaster LM311 on 12/13 July 1943. The aircraft was severely damaged by flak over Turin and on return to base suffered structural failure when flaps were lowered, breaking in two. Chapman reported having no elevator control and was flying on trimmer only.
key_details: Returning from bombing raid on Turin
Flak damage forward of tail section
Structural failure when flaps lowered for landing
Pilot reported no elevator control, flying on trimmer
Three of 18 squadron aircraft lost that night

external_narratives.summary: Comprehensive local history account of Lancaster LM311's last flight from RAF Bottesford with 467 (RAAF) Squadron. Documents the seven crew members and the circumstances of the crash near Long Bennington village.
key_details: Took off at 2248 hrs on 12 July 1943

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for the memorial to the crew of Lancaster LM311 at Long Bennington.

Research article on the last flight of LM311, known as 'L-Liz'beth'. Details the crew composition: P/O Chapman (RAAF pilot), Sgt Michaels (navigator), Sgt Smith (bomb aimer), Sgt Donlevy (W/Op AG), Sgt Greenwood (flight engineer), F/Sgt Bruce (mid-upper gunner), Sgt Buchanan (rear gunner).
key_details: Sgt Norman Campbell Smith was bomb aimer, age 21

external_narratives.summary: CWGC story page for Sgt Norman Campbell Smith, navigator/bomber with 467 RAAF Squadron, who died aged 21 on 13 July 1943 aboard Lancaster LM311.
key_details: Sgt Norman Campbell Smith age 21 at death

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
